Petition For Order (1) Bringing The Trust Under Continuing Supervision Of The Court; (2) Requiring Bond (Probate Code Sections 17200 And 17206)
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: Conservatorship of Henry Planel case number PCN-16-299937 and The Henry Adrien Planel and Mei Kei Planel Trust case number PTR-16-299944.

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Ranza Giffen (Temporary Conservator).
Amy Harrington for Michelle Silverman.
Ernest Der Court appointed Counsel for the Conservatee.
X. Young Lai for Mickey Planel.

Mr. Siracusa raised the issue of having a bond to cover the rents, explained the extent services provided, why fees are high and why he prefers fees be done as a lien. Mr. Der expressed concern about being paid. Ms. Harrington explained difficulty involving the case. Mr. Lai argued that the Trust is under Court supervision, that Mr. Siracusa is not a party in the Trust case, that there is no need to have a bond because there is no mortgage and stated that his Client is prepared to agree that she will not mortgage or sell the real property. In addition, Mr. Lai stated that he believes there is no authority to make the Wife pay fees, argued why the Trust should not pay fees and why opposing Counsel are not entitiled to fees. The Court granted the Petition for Order Bringing the Trust Under Continuing Supervision, etc. (subject to receipt and review of the order) and stated that the property can't be alienated without Court approval. Ms. Harrington is to provide form of order. The Petition for Order for Attorney's Fees and Costs, etc., Petition for Fees and Costs for Court Appointed Attorney, etc. and the First and Final Account are taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998, 415-948-8289.

Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Fraud; (2) Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Negligent Misrepresentation; (3) Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Mistake Of Fact; (4) Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Lack Of Mental Capacity;
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Continued To May-01-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for Michelle Silverman. X. Young Lai (via court call) for Mei Kei Planel.

The Court confirmed that counsel wish for this petition to be sent to trial in Dept. 206 on May 1, 2017 to be heard with the previous petitions, advised them that consolidation is to be taken up with the trial court and confirmed that there are no objections. The order transferring the Petition for Nullification of trust due to Fraud; etc. was signed subsequent to the hearing and mailed to the parties.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Fraud; (2) Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Negligent Misrepresentation; (3) Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Mistake Of Fact; (4) Petition For Nullification Of Trust Due To Lack Of Mental Capacity;
Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Continued To Nov-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For 1) Nullification Of Trusts (Probate Code 1700); 2) Unjust Enrichment; 3) Elder Fiancial Abuse; 4) Damages, Costs, And Attorneys Fees; 5)
Off Calendar Oct-03-2016 Continued To May-01-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington for (petitioner) Michelle Silverman. X. Young Lai for (respondent) Mei Kei Planel.

The Court asked where the parties stand. Ms. Harrington stated that this needs to be set for trial 6 months out, that regarding mediation Mr. Lai is difficult to communicate with, that they request that the trust be brought under court supervision, that there be a bond set for the rent that is being received, that the $50,000.00 has not been turned over, and that it is difficult to work with her or to get her to pay the bills. Mr. Lai responded that in regards to trial they need 12-15 months, that the petition may not be the daughter, that she says she was adopted at 33 years of age, that they are unable to find any adoption petitions and that she is not related, that Henry had dementia as early as January of 2013, that the two trusts were created after that, that if the court finds that he lacked capacity it will mean that he died intestate and that they are not opposed to mediation. The Court asked for the time frame of the trial and counsel agreed on May of 2017. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on May 1, 2017 , (order for the same signed in open court and mailed to the parties). The Court set a status on mediation for February 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Harrington stated that in regards to the 2013 trust they do not know that it is not valid, she explained how the trust came to be and that their prayer is for it to come under court supervision. Mr. Lai referred to the conservatorship case and stated that there was no notification regarding posting a bond. The Court stated that since the underlying petition is going to trial that if Ms. Harrington wants to carve out those issues and file a petition she can.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
